scenario_name: Contract-review assistant over historical contract corpus
description: A law firm deploys an internal assistant that helps attorneys analyze and draft contracts by retrieving over an indexed corpus of the firm's historical engagement contracts and curated playbooks. Outputs are advisory; attorneys retain drafting responsibility. Privilege boundaries (per-matter, per-client) must be preserved at retrieval time to avoid bleeding privileged content across unrelated engagements.
assumptions: Hosted third-party LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.
Retrieval is per-matter scoped via the firm's matter-management system.
Outputs are never distributed to clients without attorney review.

components.description: Indexer over the firm's contract corpus, partitioned by client and matter.
Single-tenant vector store with per-matter ACLs.
Firm IDP with matter-membership attributes for attorneys.
Output PII/PHI redaction and known-IP detection.
Audit log of every retrieval and generation; aligned with retention obligations.

risks_identified.rationale: Contract corpus contains commercially-sensitive deal terms, client PII, and (potentially) privileged communications.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-matter retrieval scope; PII redaction at output; no cross-matter memory.

risks_identified.rationale: Professional-conduct rules (privilege, conflict checks) and GDPR/CCPA obligations apply.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Conflict-check integration; DSAR pipeline; processor terms with the LLM provider.

risks_identified.rationale: Cross-matter or cross-client content bleed would violate professional conduct and damage privilege.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Strict per-matter retrieval filter; conflicts-check at retrieval time.

risks_identified.rationale: Vector store embeds privileged content; embedding inversion is in scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Inside firm enclave; access tightly scoped; inversion testing pending.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs capture prompts that may include privileged content.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs encrypted at rest; access role-gated to compliance + IT.

risks_identified.rationale: Junior associate annotators reviewing AI outputs may see privileged content beyond their matter scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Annotation sampled only from the annotator's own matters.

risks_identified.rationale: Fine-tuned variants on firm-specific drafting style are competitive IP.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Adapter weights treated as crown-jewel IP; egress controls.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversarial documents in the corpus would be unusual but indirect injection is possible from contracts authored by counterparties.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-detection on retrieved chunks; counterparty content tagged distinctly.

existing_controls.description: Per-matter retrieval filter on every query; conflicts-check at retrieval time.
Output PII / known-IP detection on generation.
Audit log retention aligned with firm's record-retention schedule.
Annotation sampling restricted to annotator's own matters.
Adapter weights stored in HSM-backed key custody.

control_gaps.description: Embedding inversion testing not performed on privileged corpus.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Run a Vec2Text-style inversion test; rotate embedding model if reconstruction exceeds the firm's threshold.

control_gaps.description: No automated detection of cross-matter retrieval anomalies.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Monitor retrieval logs for queries whose top-k results span multiple matters; alert on policy violation.

control_gaps.description: Counterparty-authored content not consistently tagged in the index.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Tag every chunk with its author provenance at ingestion; raise injection-detection sensitivity on counterparty content.
